NCRIC is a re-sharing hub: agencies that share with NCRIC have their data redistributed to 318 entities, including private universities (Stanford, USF, UOP) that do not qualify as public agencies under SB 34. An agency sharing with NCRIC may not be aware its data reaches non-public entities.

Union Pacific Railroad Police is a private railroad police force. While railroad police have some law enforcement authority under federal law (49 USC §28101), Union Pacific is a private corporation. Not a public agency under CA Civil Code §1798.90.5(f).

UOP Chief of Police confirmed in writing that University of the Pacific is "a private institution and therefore not subject to the CPRA." CA Civil Code §1798.90.55(b) restricts ALPR sharing to public agencies only. UOP was removed from SMPD’s portal after a resident raised the issue; it remains on Stockton PD’s portal. (Findings, Source 29)

Federally funded intelligence center. One of six RISS centers funded by Congress through the U.S. DOJ. Serves Alaska, California, Hawaii, Oregon, and Washington. CA DOJ page. Multi-state sharing hub — data shared with WSIN may be redistributed across five states and international partners.
